description: All character sprites: player, NPCs, and boss sprites. Drawn with ctx.fillRect in PixelWorldEngine.tsx and BossBattle.tsx.

backlog.title: LYSO boss sprite redesign — 3-phase HP states
acceptance: Phase 1 (100-67% HP): bright purple oval with visible double membrane, 8 spiked appendages in circular orbit, single glowing red eye center, acid drip from base
Phase 2 (66-33% HP): cracks visible as dark diagonal lines across membrane body, eye becomes split/jagged, 2 of 8 spikes visibly broken off (shortened), acid leaks through cracks as green pixels
Phase 3 (<33% HP): membrane mostly broken — only 4-5 top-row rects remain, 3 red eyes visible through gaps, spikes jagged/asymmetric, continuous acid drip animation
All phases: top-left lighting convention applied (top rows brighter than bottom rows)
At SCALE=3 on 720x540 canvas, boss reads as threatening at 3 meters viewing distance
Score >= 8.0 on QUALITY-RUBRIC.json silhouetteScore criterion
backlog.biologicalBasis: Lysosome: spherical membrane-bound organelle, acidic interior (pH 4.5-5), contains hydrolytic enzymes. Rogue lysosome concept: membrane integrity loss = acid release = danger.
backlog.notes: Current implementation (rows of width data producing oval) is correct approach. Needs: (1) wider max width for stronger silhouette, (2) membrane detail via alternating light/dark rows, (3) geometry changes per HP phase, not just tint changes, (4) base acid pool as 3-pixel spread beneath.

backlog.title: VIRON boss sprite redesign — icosahedral virus 3-phase
acceptance: Phase 1: clear icosahedron silhouette — 5 visible triangular face planes constructed from rects, each face differently shaded using shiftColor()
6 spike proteins protruding from vertices (3px long rects at 6 cardinal+diagonal angles)
3 orbiting nucleocapsid rings: each ring is 8 dots at different radii, animated via Math.cos/sin
Single green glowing eye (receptor-binding site metaphor) at front face center
Phase 2: two spike proteins visibly broken (shorter), one face plane cracked (dark diagonal pixels)
Phase 3: half the icosahedron geometry missing, core exposed as dark green sphere, all remaining spikes glitching (alternating between long/short every 2 frames)
Score >= 8.0 on silhouetteScore criterion
backlog.biologicalBasis: Bacteriophage / icosahedral capsid virus. T4 phage is the visual inspiration: hexagonal head, tail fiber proteins as spikes. DNA injection mechanism = the attack animation.
backlog.notes: Current implementation: overlapping rectangles produce rough icosahedron shape. Problem: at SCALE=3 the overlapping creates muddy visual. Solution: explicitly draw 5 face planes as colored rect clusters, then add spike rects at fixed angles. The orbital rings are the strongest current element — keep but increase radius and particle count.

backlog.title: OVERFIT boss sprite redesign — neural network skull 3-phase
acceptance: Phase 1: large skull shape (wider than current, 32px wide at jaw) with neural network circuitry visible as glowing lines ON the skull surface
Left eye socket: purple (overfitted data), Right eye socket: cyan (validation data — 'ERROR')
Mouth: binary code 010101 in alternating lit/unlit pixel rows, flashes open/shut every 30 frames
8 neural connection nodes visible: pulsing dots at skull exterior, connected by thin 1px lines
Phase 2: validation eye goes dark (ERROR state), skull starts showing static (alternating dark/light 1x1 pixels in 20% of skull area), one node disconnects and falls
Phase 3: heavy static on 50% of skull, only 3 nodes remain, mouth shows only '0000' (total overfit failure), purple eye is HUGE (obsessively overfit), cyan eye is gone
Score >= 8.0 on silhouetteScore criterion
backlog.biologicalBasis: Overfitted neural network: memorizes training data perfectly, fails on validation. Visual metaphor: a neural network that has 'become' the data — its skull IS the training set. The more it knows, the more rigid and broken it becomes.
backlog.notes: This is the most conceptually interesting boss. The current implementation has the right ingredients but is too small. The skull should use the top 60% of the boss canvas area. Neural nodes should be 4x4 game pixels (vs current 8x8 canvas pixels). The 'ERROR ERROR ERROR' moment should be shown as pixel-text in the eye socket.

backlog.title: AMYLOID TYRANT boss sprite redesign — fibril accumulation 3-phase
acceptance: Phase 1: clear beta-sheet stack silhouette — 6 horizontal sheets of increasing width toward base (top: 8px, base: 26px), each sheet separated by 2px dark gap, alternating light/dark sheet colors
12 fibril tendrils growing upward from top sheet — each tendril is 2px wide, height 8-16px, animated to grow/shrink via sin(t + fibrilIndex)
Two amber eye rectangles on front face (6x4 game pixels each) with dark pupils
Phase 2: 3 of 6 sheets visibly cracked (dark diagonal crack rects), fibrils now 16-24px and some droop sideways, eyes glow brighter
Phase 3: sheets fragment into individual 2x2 blocks arranged roughly in sheet positions, fibrils are 24-32px and completely cover the upper canvas area, eyes become 8x6 (engorged)
PERSONALITY SIGNAL: the accumulation. Everything gets more fibril-covered over time. Score >= 8.0 silhouetteScore.
backlog.biologicalBasis: Amyloid fibrils: beta-sheet stacked protein aggregates. Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, prion diseases. The tyranny is the accumulation — once started, it cannot be stopped. Beta sheets stack horizontally in parallel.
backlog.notes: Current implementation gets the beta-sheet stack right but the gray color reads as stone/rock, not protein. Proteins are more ivory/warm-white. The fibril animation (sin wave per fibril) is the saving grace — increase fibril count from 12 to 16 and vary heights more dramatically.

backlog.title: Player character redesign — 32x20 game pixels, 4-frame walk cycle
acceptance: Dimensions: 10 wide x 20 tall game pixels (current is ~10x20, acceptable but needs stronger silhouette)
Silhouette: visible head-to-body taper (head wider than shoulders), no floating rectangle look
Walk cycle: 4 frames. Frame 0: neutral stance. Frame 1: left foot forward, right arm forward. Frame 2: neutral (crossed). Frame 3: right foot forward, left arm forward.
Direction: down (face visible), up (back visible — NOT just hair shift), left (side profile with one eye), right (side profile mirrored)
Avatar customization maintained: skinColor, clothColor, hairColor params unchanged
Hair: drawn as a proper hat/cap shape, not just a thin bar above head
Shoes: dark-colored 3x2 game pixel blocks visible beneath legs
Score >= 7.5 on QUALITY-RUBRIC.json (Sprint 3 target, not Sprint 1)
backlog.biologicalBasis: N/A — player character is a human student entering the biological world
backlog.notes: Current 2-frame walk cycle (bob up/down) is a vertical head-bob only. True 4-frame RPG walk cycle: feet alternate left/right with torso counter-rotation. The legShift mechanic is the right idea but currently creates a uniform 1px vertical shift. Need: left leg forward + right arm forward as linked movement.

backlog.title: Elliot NPC in-world sprite — match mascot personality
acceptance: Curly hair: implemented as multiple small rect clusters, not just a wide rectangle at top
Glasses: two 3x2 white-ish rectangles with 1px black border drawn inside head area
Mismatched socks: left sock red (#ff4444), right sock yellow (#ffee44) — must be visible at SCALE=3
Lab coat: white/light gray body, visible lapel lines (2 dark diagonal rects)
Idle animation: alternating between frame 0 (looking forward) and frame 1 (slight glasses-push gesture — arm moves up 1px)
Score >= 7.5 on styleConsistency criterion (must match SVG mascot aesthetic)
backlog.notes: Current implementation is good — mismatched socks are present, lab coat is readable. Main gap: hair is drawn as `gr2(ox, oy, 1, 0, 10, 4, '#2a1a0a')` which is a solid rectangle. Should use 3-4 small overlapping rects to suggest curls.

backlog.title: Ben NPC in-world sprite — sandwich and cargo pocket detail
acceptance: Sandwich: 3-layer stack (bread top, green lettuce, bread bottom) in right hand, sways with walk cycle using armSwing variable
Cargo pants: 2 visible pocket rects per leg, slightly darker than main pants color
Hair: short, dark, tousled — 3 small rect clumps slightly offset from each other
Build: slightly wider than Elliot — 12px wide vs 10px (the 'well-fed bioinformatician')
Idle animation: sandwich moves up/down 1px with breathing animation (every 2 animFrame ticks)
Score >= 7.5 on styleConsistency criterion
backlog.notes: Current implementation already has the sandwich. Main gap: body width is same as Elliot — Ben should be visually bulkier to differentiate the characters at a glance.

backlog.title: Alex NPC in-world sprite — turtleneck and coffee precision
acceptance: Turtleneck collar: 3px tall rect at neck, same dark color as body, collar must visually bridge head and body
Coffee cup: 3x5 game pixel rect in left hand, two-tone (paper beige exterior, dark coffee top), handle as 1x2 rect protruding right
Hair: slick — a flat cap shape (rectangle perfectly aligned), no texture. Communicates 'precise person.'
Build: slightly taller and thinner than others — 10px wide, 22px tall
Idle: coffee cup tilts 1px down every 4 animFrames (drinking action)
Score >= 7.5 on styleConsistency criterion
backlog.notes: Alex is the most visually understated character — all black, precise. The in-world sprite should lean into this with straight lines and no organic curves. The coffee detail is the only 'warm' element.

backlog.title: Henry NPC in-world sprite — holographic CRT effect
acceptance: CRT scanline effect: every other row of Henry's body is drawn at 70% opacity (globalAlpha), creating visible horizontal gaps that simulate a holographic projection
Geometric grid lines: horizontal and vertical 1px rects drawn at 30% opacity across body area
Eyes: bright white 2x2 rects (no iris — pure energy), slight glow created by surrounding 4x4 area filled at 20% opacity in cyan
Flicker system: THREE opacity states cycling: 0.4 / 0.65 / 0.55, toggled by `frame % 3`
Outer halo: 16x24 game pixel area (larger than sprite) at 5% opacity, giving aura
Score >= 8.0 on styleConsistency criterion — this is the most distinctive NPC, must excel
backlog.notes: Current implementation already does the globalAlpha flicker correctly. Main gap: the CRT scanline effect (alternating row opacity) is NOT currently implemented — the body is drawn as solid rects. Adding alternating row opacity would dramatically improve the holographic read.

backlog.title: Player character — idle animation frames
acceptance: Idle frame 0: neutral stance, arms at sides
Idle frame 1: weight shift — body moves 1px right, left foot 1px forward
Idle frame 2: look up — eyes move 1px up in head
Idle frame 3: return neutral
Triggered when: player is stationary for > 500ms
Uses animFrame clock (not walkFrame)
backlog.notes: Idle animation is a polish item for Sprint 3 only after the core walk cycle redesign (SPR-005) is complete.

backlog.title: Boss projectile sprites — per-realm attack visuals
acceptance: LYSO (realm 1): acid blob — irregular green shape, 3x3 game pixels with 1px drips from corners
VIRON (realm 2): DNA strand segment — 4x2 rect in blue/green alternating base-pair colors
OVERFIT (realm 3): binary stream — 4x4 rect showing '01' text in pixels, leaving a trailing streak
AMYLOID TYRANT (realm 4): fibril shard — 2x6 gray rect slightly rotated effect via rect stacking
Each projectile has a distinct silhouette readable at 6x6 canvas pixels
backlog.notes: Current projectile for all realms is an orange 10x10 circle approximation (5x5 game pixels). This is the same for all four bosses which breaks realm identity.

backlog.title: Boss hit particle effects — per-realm VFX
acceptance: LYSO hit: 16 green acid particles + 4 purple membrane shards
VIRON hit: 12 cyan particles + 4 spike fragments (elongated 1x3 rects)
OVERFIT hit: 20 particles cycling through purple/cyan/white (neural network colors)
AMYLOID hit: 8 gray fibril particles falling straight down (gravity-weighted)
Victory burst: 60 particles in realm accent colors, radial spread from boss center
All particles: 6x6 canvas pixel squares fading over 0.8s (current is correct)
backlog.notes: Current particle system is generic rainbow colors for all realms. The particle colors should match the realm palette. Only the victory burst rainbow is justified (celebration overrides palette).

backlog.title: Avatar customization preview — update AvatarRenderer.tsx
acceptance: Avatar preview updates in real-time as player selects skin/cloth/hair
Preview renders same drawPlayer() function used in-game (single source of truth)
Preview shows all 4 walk cycle frames as a sprite sheet for player to see
All 4 direction states shown in a 2x2 grid preview
Score >= 7.5 on readabilityAtSize criterion at the small preview size
backlog.notes: AvatarRenderer.tsx exists but its implementation is unknown — needs audit before starting this task.

backlog.title: Boss intro cutscene — boss entrance animation frame
acceptance: During 'intro' phase: boss descends from top of canvas over 2 seconds (y position animated)
Boss starts at y=-60, moves to y=CH*0.40 (its battle position)
Landing impact: 8 particles burst outward on landing
Screen flash on landing: 3-frame white flash
After landing: boss enters idle bob animation (sin wave ±4px over 2s period)
backlog.notes: Currently the boss appears immediately at the start of the intro phase. An entrance animation significantly increases player tension before the dialogue begins.

backlog.title: Boss defeated state — death animation
acceptance: On victory: boss explodes into 50+ particles over 0.5s
Boss sprite during explosion: pieces drift apart (4-5 independent rect groups, each moving with a different velocity vector)
After 0.5s of explosion: boss fully replaced by particles, no main sprite visible
During final 1s before victory transition: screen dims, particles decelerate
Each boss has realm-appropriate explosion colors
backlog.notes: Currently victory just dims the screen and shows '★ VICTORY! ★' text while the boss sprite remains. The boss should visibly disintegrate.

backlog.title: Player hurt animation — damage taken feedback
acceptance: On wrong answer: player sprite appears in bottom-left of battle canvas (small, 6x10 game pixels)
Player sprite flashes red for 0.5s when hit
Player sprite uses avatar skin/cloth/hair colors from store
At <50% HP: player sprite tilts 5px to one side (wounded posture)
At 1 HP remaining: player sprite flickers at 4Hz
backlog.notes: Currently player damage is communicated only via heart icons and red flash overlay. Adding a small player sprite grounds the player in the battle visually. BitBio doesn't have an Undertale-style soul — the player IS in the battle.

backlog.title: NPC 'surprise' animation — approach indicator
acceptance: When player is within 2 tiles of an NPC: NPC frame switches to 'notice' pose
Notice pose: character head turns toward player (direction-aware), eyebrows raise
Small '!' exclamation above NPC head (3x5 game pixels in white, 1px dark border)
NPC returns to idle after player moves away or starts dialogue
backlog.notes: Currently there is only the `▲ E` text prompt near NPCs. A visual 'notice' pose makes the world feel more alive.

backlog.title: Boss phase transition animation — visual damage staging
acceptance: At HP crossing 66%: 0.3s stagger animation — boss shakes 10px left/right rapidly
At HP crossing 33%: 0.5s stagger + 20 particles burst from boss center
Phase transition tracked in BossState (new field: lastPhaseTransition)
During stagger: boss sprite shows phase N+1 (damaged state) flickering in at 50% opacity
After stagger: full transition to damaged sprite
backlog.notes: Phase transitions currently happen silently — HP changes and the color tint changes, but there is no visual punctuation. Phase transitions should FEEL impactful.

backlog.title: Mascot SVG sprite sheet — high-resolution export specs
acceptance: Document the exact SVG spec for each mascot in assets/sprites/
Spec includes: viewBox, all rect/path/ellipse elements with exact coordinates
Note discrepancies between SVG mascots and canvas NPC sprites
Determine which version (SVG or canvas) should be considered canonical
Recommendation: SVG for UI/mascot panels, canvas for in-world NPCs — document this clearly
backlog.notes: The SVG mascots (MascotDisplay.tsx) are significantly more detailed than the canvas in-world NPCs. This is intentional (different rendering contexts) but must be explicitly documented to prevent confusion.

backlog.title: Boss HP bar — segment markers and skull icon
acceptance: HP bar has 10 segment dividers (thin 1px dark vertical lines at each 10% interval)
Segment at 66%: double-width divider marking phase 1/2 transition
Segment at 33%: double-width divider marking phase 2/3 transition
Skull icon (5x5 game pixels) at left end of HP bar
Skull icon: 3 rects wide oval, 2 dark rects for eye sockets, 3 rect 'teeth' row below
Score >= 7.5 on readabilityAtSize criterion
backlog.notes: Current HP bar is clean but generic. Segment markers communicate the phase system visually, preparing the player for the phase transition moments.

backlog.title: Player heart display redesign — pixel-art heart shape
acceptance: Heart shape using fillRect approximation: 3 rects per heart forming recognizable heart silhouette
Layout: top-left: 2x2, top-right: 2x2 (separated by 1px gap), below: 4x2 center, tapering to point
Full heart: bright red (#ff2244), with 1px highlight at top-left (#ff6666)
Empty heart: dark red (#441122), no highlight
Hearts pulse (scale ±0.5px) when player takes damage
Score >= 8.0 on readabilityAtSize criterion — hearts are critical UI
backlog.notes: Current hearts are pure filled rectangles (20x18px canvas). At SCALE=3 this is approximately 7x6 game pixels — enough room for a proper heart silhouette using 5-6 rects.

backlog.title: Boss name plate — proper pixel-art banner
acceptance: Current CSS text-based name plate replaced with canvas-drawn version
Dark red banner (12x4 game pixels) with double-border (1px bright red outside, 1px dark red inside)
Boss name rendered as pixel text using custom 5x7 pixel font or current monospace
Banner position: top-center of boss canvas area
Phase indicators: small dot for each remaining HP phase, dims as phases are defeated
backlog.notes: Current name plate is HTML div with CSS border — works fine for now but canvas-drawn version would be more visually consistent with the pixel art aesthetic.
